title: Resources and tools
text:
This repo is intended to be a living document for SWG and IAT members to share useful information and resources related to Open Science, programming and Data Visualisation.
Some documents that provide guiding principles:
Check out the Open Science Awesome List

title: Resources and tools
text:
Data visualisation

Fundamentals of Data Visualization
Open Visualisation Academy

text:
R
What they forgot to teach you about R: https://rstats.wtf/

title: Resources and tools
text:
Quarto

Beautiful slides with RevealJS and Quarto

title: Resources and tools
text:
Tools and Software

EU alternatives for software - A directory of software alternatives to popular proprietary software, with a focus on European users and organizations.
gettingstuffdoneai - Directory of Generative AI Tools and Models, categorized by use case and functionality.
Inkscape - Open source vector graphics editor, alternative to Adobe products, useful for creating and editing figures for publications and presentations. Available via the ZALF baramundi client.

title: Resources and tools
text:
Training

Digital Research Academy - a trainer network focused on Open Science, Data Literacy, and Research Software Engineering (DRA’s Zenodo collection).
BERD academy
BERD reproducible research course
NFDI4Earth - Educational Portal
Assured Training -Ensures the safe, legal, and ethical use of sensitive data through targeted training and certification.
KI-campus - Numerous free courses on AI and related topics, including data science, machine learning, and ethical considerations in AI.

title: Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs
text:
Discuss your project with us
Welcome to the website of the Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs (DMI) Working Group. We are a service working group within the Innovation Center for Agricultural System Transformation (IAT) as part of the Liebniz Center for Agricultural Landscape Research (ZALF). Our mission is to support researchers and partners within the IAT with all things related to data and modelling. We have represented the different ways that we do this in the graphic below:

title: DaMIfLL Presents at IAT Kickoff Meeting
text:
Members of the DaMIfLL working group attended and presented at the IAT kickoff meeting. The presentation introduced the DaMIfLL initiative, outlined our working group structure, and highlighted ongoing efforts to improve data management and interoperability in land use and landscape research.
We look forward to continued collaboration with the IAT community.

title: MonksHillLabs Initiative
text:
MonksHillLabs is a collaborative research platform that provides shared computational infrastructure, data repositories, and development environments for landscape and land use modelling projects.
The initiative brings together researchers, data scientists, and software developers to co-create tools, standards, and workflows that advance data interoperability and reproducible research in landscape science.
Current activities include developing standardized data formats, building modular modelling components, and hosting hackathons and training workshops.

title: Living Lab Dashboards
text:
The Living Lab Dashboards project develops interactive web-based tools for visualizing and exploring data from living lab experiments conducted across our research network.
These dashboards integrate diverse data streams including environmental sensors, field observations, and remote sensing data to provide real-time insights into landscape dynamics and experimental outcomes.
The platform supports collaborative analysis and data-driven decision making for adaptive management of living lab sites.

title: Upcoming Workshop and Hackathon at iEMSs 2025
text:
We are excited to announce that DaMIfLL will host a workshop and hackathon at the International Environmental Modelling and Software Society (iEMSs) 2025 conference.
The event will bring together researchers and practitioners to collaboratively tackle data interoperability challenges in landscape and land use modelling. Participants will work hands-on with datasets and tools in a hackathon format.
More details on registration and the programme will follow soon.

title: DaMIfLL at the FAIRagro Community Summit
text:
Working group members attended the FAIRagro community summit, joining researchers and data infrastructure specialists to discuss FAIR data principles in the agricultural and landscape sciences.
The event provided valuable opportunities to exchange ideas, identify common challenges, and explore potential collaborations between DaMIfLL and FAIRagro initiatives.

You can read more about licenses in the book itself.
